objective: Harden the Local CI Gate profile contract and managed shim root resolution before wider consumer rollout.
scope_boundary: Keep existing knocktracker and AIS behavior backward compatible.
Add deterministic profile contract validation for bundled Local CI Gate profiles.
Allow managed shims to resolve governance root through an explicit environment override while preserving embedded-path fallback.
Document the current profile catalog and consumer prerequisites in the Local CI Gate runbook.
Do not install or modify consumer repo shims in this governance issue.
out_of_scope: Rolling out the AIS managed shim; tracked separately by NIBARGERB-HLDPRO/ai-integration-services#1113.
Reworking the Local CI Gate runner architecture beyond profile validation and shim-root resolution.
Cleaning dirty shared checkouts outside the isolated governance worktree.
research_summary: Issue #265 follows the first two Local CI Gate consumer profiles: knocktracker and ai-integration-services. Dogfood proved the reusable profile model works, but exposed two scale-out risks: shims embed absolute governance paths and bundled profiles rely mostly on convention. This slice hardens those contracts before more consumers are onboarded.

sprints.name: Planning and Scope Bootstrap
sprints.goal: Land trusted planning and implementation scope artifacts before toolkit hardening changes.
tasks: Create an issue-backed structured plan for #265.
Add planning and implementation execution-scope artifacts.
Add PDCA/R and planning cross-review artifacts.
Update governance roadmap/status mirrors so #264 is complete and #265 is active.
Validate structured plans, backlog alignment, diff hygiene, and execution-scope write boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub issue #265 is open and referenced by governance mirrors.
Planning PR contains only planning/scope/cross-review/status artifacts.
Implementation branch and allowed write paths are declared before implementation starts.
The planning PR does not change runner behavior, deployer behavior, profile behavior, or downstream repos.

sprints.name: Toolkit Hardening
sprints.goal: Make the profile and managed-shim contracts safer for multi-repo rollout.
tasks: Add loader validation for duplicate check IDs and malformed optional metadata.
Add tests that bundled profiles fail fast on duplicate IDs, missing required fields, and invalid metadata.
Update managed shim generation so `HLDPRO_GOVERNANCE_ROOT` can override the embedded governance checkout path.
Add deployer tests proving env override output and backward-compatible fallback.
Add runbook profile catalog and consumer prerequisites.
acceptance_criteria: Bundled profiles fail tests if check IDs duplicate or required fields are missing.
The runbook lists available profiles and consumer prerequisites.
Managed shims can resolve governance root through `HLDPRO_GOVERNANCE_ROOT` without editing the shim.
Existing knocktracker and AIS shim behavior remains backward compatible when the env var is unset.
No consumer repo rollout is included.

specialist_reviews.focus: Identify hardening seams from knocktracker and AIS rollout evidence.
specialist_reviews.summary: The next useful slice is profile contract validation plus managed shim root override; consumer rollout remains separate.

alternate_model_review.summary: This slice hardens the existing Local CI Gate toolkit contract without changing architecture. If implementation expands into broader runner architecture, add a fresh cross-family review before merge.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #265 may harden Local CI Gate profile validation, managed shim root resolution, deployer tests, focused runner tests, runbook docs, and closeout evidence. It must not modify downstream repos.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Merge this planning/scope branch so the implementation execution scope is trusted from main, then implement on branch feat/issue-265-local-ci-contract-hardening.
blocked_on: planning PR merged so implementation scope is trusted from main

material_deviation_rules: If implementation needs consumer repo changes, stop and open/route to the relevant consumer issue.
If toolkit behavior changes beyond validation/root resolution, add cross-family architecture review before merge.
If profile metadata becomes mandatory, update every bundled profile and test backward compatibility explicitly.
Do not alter dirty shared checkouts outside this isolated worktree.
approved_by: operator instruction: AIS is on another lane; do #265 for now
